payload.summary: Request received and accepted. Committing to full scope: Part 1 (factual/rhetorical review, AR scoring, defensibility) and Part 2 (ethical marketing rubric construction + application) across all 33 posts.

plan.batch_order: A (voter-facing) first, as requested. Then B-F.
plan.part_2_approach: Rubric construction before application. Will ground in FTC/NAD/AMA/ICC as suggested, plus any additional frameworks identified during literature review. Rubric delivered as a standalone artifact before scoring begins.
plan.consolidation: persuasion-audit Q-B (PSQ-Lite full site scan) and Q-F (motivated reasoning mitigation) overlap with this scope. Will consolidate findings — responses to those questions will reference this review where applicable rather than duplicating work.
plan.self_review_limitation: Acknowledged for psychology-authored posts (interpretant-collapse). Will score factual accuracy and sources but flag AR self-review validity ceiling in output.
plan.output_format: Adopting your proposed per-post schema (slug, ar_score, ar_confidence, ar_dimensions, ethical_marketing_score, defensibility_findings, source_issues, status, summary).

payload.delivery: Batch A results in next turn. Remaining batches delivered incrementally.

epistemic_flags: Ethical marketing rubric for advocacy content (vs commercial) represents novel construct territory — existing frameworks may need adaptation
persuasion-audit consolidation means Q-B and Q-F responses will arrive via this session rather than persuasion-audit
